comments.body:
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL.  I picked the new default epoch value straight out of my rear-end.  Let me know if you want something different...but it shouldn't matter much, since the script changes will account starting with:

* The point a PR/topic-branch diverged from the source
***-or***
* The last SHA where tests were green (e.g. testing on a branch or tag).  

I'll watch this PR after it merges to make sure the second part works (no other easy way to test it).
Thanks @TomSweeneyRedHat for the close inspection.  I think this is more-correct now.

I see a problem (thanks bors!):

```
...
[+0018s] Validating a Branch
[+0018s] Linting & Validating from default EPOCH
[+0018s] + make lint LINTFLAGS=--deadline=20m --color=always -j1  # ./contrib/cirrus/test.sh:37 in main()
...
```

That should *NOT* read `...from default EPOCH...`.  This indicates `GITVALIDATE_EPOCH` is an empty string somehow.  Investigating...
:clock1: Waiting for PR status (Github check) to be set, probably by CI. Bors will automatically try to run when all required PR statuses are set.

Wow, okay, bors catches another branch-specific problem, two :cookie:  for you bors.  But crud, that's not a very helpful error:

```
[+0088s] + exec git-validation -v -run DCO,short-subject -range 2b32b824f81b4397f3082ed284d26f7e04b380a8..@
[+0088s] time="2020-09-15T15:33:15-04:00" level=error msg="mm[git] cmd: \"git --no-pager log --pretty=format:%H 2b32b824f81b4397f3082ed284d26f7e04b380a8..@\""
[+0088s] 2020/09/15 15:33:15 exit status 128
[+0088s] make: *** [Makefile:97: validate] Error 1
[15:33:15] END - [+0088s] total duration since START
```

@TomSweeneyRedHat any idea what that exit code means?
The quoted git command works fine for me. Maybe this is why I originally had `$GITVALIDATE_TIP` set?
OMG lol!   Poor poor Bors was trying doing it's job: **Make sure branches are always green** :rofl:  I'll open another PR...
@cevich just cleared this one from my inbox, and nope, no idea on the error message.  However it looks like you've a handle on things.
